Five finalist identities for the first clinical-grade skin recovery system. Presented for ownership review & selection.

Recover Beautifully.

The Brief

One brand. Five ways
to say trust me with your skin.

Each finalist below was engineered against the same strategic brief: clinical authority with feminine warmth — a mark that earns a place on a dermatologist's shelf and in Sarah's beach bag. Every candidate is shown with in-context variations and photographed on the two hero product formats: the Recovery Mist™ spray bottle and the OPC DermMist™ cream tube.

The Finalists

Logo Candidates

01

The Lotus

Geometric Emblem · Symbolic

On Navy
On Blush
At Scale

Why it works: the unfolding geometric lotus signals renewal and layered healing — skin literally rebuilding itself. Strong standalone icon potential for packaging, favicon and app use.

On Product
02

The Classic

DM Serif Wordmark · Editorial

On Navy
On Blush
At Scale

Why it works: high-contrast serif "derma" delivers pharmaceutical gravitas; the hand-drawn swash S adds the human touch. Fully vectorized — production-ready today.

On Product
03

The Modernist

Bold Sans Wordmark · Confident

On Navy
On Blush
At Scale

Why it works: the SkinCeuticals playbook — bold, clinical sans-serif that reads instantly at any size. Maximum legibility on shelf and screen. Fully vectorized.

On Product
04

The Emblem

Circle Crest + Serif · Prestige

On Navy
On Blush
At Scale

Why it works: the encircled cupped-hand-and-bloom crest carries Tatcha-level ceremony. The emblem detaches beautifully as a standalone seal for caps, tissue paper and social avatars.

On Product
05

The Refined

Hairline Serif + Crest · Quiet Luxury

On Navy
On Blush
At Scale

Why it works: the lightest, most editorial cut — hairline serif elegance in the La Mer tradition, paired with the crest and coral script. Whispers luxury instead of shouting it.

On Product
Chromatic Strategy

The Color Palette

Color is the first clinical claim a customer reads — before a single word. The DermaSoZo palette is engineered to say medical trust, feminine warmth, visible recovery in one glance.

#0B6E6E

Deep Teal

Primary · Clinical Authority

Teal sits precisely between blue (medicine, trust) and green (nature, healing) — the exact intersection DermaSoZo occupies. It's the color of scrubs and spa water at once: calm, antiseptic, credible.

TrustCalmExpertiseBalance
#E8C4B8

Soft Blush

Secondary · Feminine Warmth

The color of healthy, recovered skin itself — the promise made visible. Blush softens the clinical edge and speaks directly to our primary female audience without drifting into "pink-washed" cliché.

ComfortSkinSoftnessCare
#E07A5F

Coral Sunset

Accent · Vital Energy

Coral is the emotional spark — sunset after a beach day, circulation returning, life coming back to skin. Used sparingly on the "Sozo" script and CTAs, it directs the eye and triggers action.

VitalityOptimismActionWarmth
#1E2A4A

Midnight Navy

Foundation · Gravitas

Navy is the boardroom of colors: authority without the coldness of black. It anchors the wordmark and gives every layout pharmaceutical weight — the reason prestige skincare leans dark blue.

AuthorityDepthPremiumStability
#FBF7F4

Ivory Silk

Canvas · Clinical Purity

Warm white — not hospital white. It keeps layouts breathable and product-photography clean while avoiding sterile coldness. Purity, safety, and space for the skin to be the hero.

PurityClaritySpaceClean
System

Working Ratio

Usage · 30 / 25 / 20 / 17 / 8

Teal leads, blush warms, coral punctuates, navy grounds, ivory breathes. Holding this ratio across packaging, web and social is what makes the brand recognizable before the logo is even read.

ConsistencyRecognitionSystem
Voice In Letterform

The Typography

Two voices, one conversation: the physician who knows, and the friend who cares. Every DermaSoZo touchpoint pairs them deliberately.

derma
AaBbCcDdEeFf 0123456789

DM Serif Display — The Physician

A high-contrast didone in the tradition of Vogue and medical journals alike. Psychologically, serifs read as established, credentialed, and time-tested — research consistently associates them with trustworthiness and expertise. The thick-to-hairline stroke contrast adds editorial luxury: this is the typeface of the diploma on the wall.

Sozo
Recover Beautifully

The Calligraphic Script — The Human Hand

"Sozo" — Greek for to heal, to restore, to make whole — is rendered by hand, never by machine. Script triggers perceptions of personal care and craftsmanship; the sweeping S and the underline flourish literally embrace the word. It is the signature on the prescription: personal, warm, human.

Clean, modern support — Karla

Karla — The Clear Communicator

Body copy, ingredient decks, and UI run in a humanist grotesque: clinical clarity without coldness. Slightly rounded terminals keep instructions friendly; generous x-height keeps 6-point drug-facts panels legible. The rule: serif for authority, script for heart, sans for facts.

Ownership Decision
